title: Nor Rel Muk Wintu Nation Federal Recognition Act
short_title: Nor Rel Muk Wintu Nation Federal Recognition Act
plain_summary: This law makes the Nor Rel Muk Wintu Nation an official Native American tribe recognized by the federal government. Once recognized, the tribe and its members can get the same services and benefits that other official tribes receive from the government. The tribe's service area will be Trinity County, California, and members can get these benefits no matter where they live or whether the tribe has a reservation. The tribe must create and keep a list of all its members and give this list to the Department of the Interior.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Nor Rel Muk Wintu Nation Federal Recognition Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Federal Recognition
sections.text:
This section explains important terms and gives federal recognition to the Nor Rel Muk Wintu Nation. The governing document means the Constitution of the Nor Rel Muk Wintu Nation that was updated and approved on September nineteenth, two thousand twenty, and any future versions. The Secretary means the Secretary of the Interior. A Tribal member means someone who was already enrolled in the Tribe when this law was passed, or someone who gets added to the membership rolls according to this law. The Tribe means the Nor Rel Muk Wintu Nation.

The federal government now officially recognizes the Tribe. All federal laws that apply to Indians, Indian Tribes, or bands of Indians will also apply to this Tribe and its members. This includes the Indian Reorganization Act from nineteen thirty four. The Tribe and its members can now receive all services and benefits that the federal government provides to other federally recognized Indian Tribes. It does not matter if the Tribe has a reservation or where Tribal members live. For the purpose of providing federal services, Trinity County in California will be considered the service area for the Tribe.

Within eighteen months of this law being passed, the Tribe must give the Secretary a membership roll with the names of all enrolled Tribal members. The requirements for being included on the membership roll must follow what is written in the Tribe's governing document. Only the Tribe has the authority and responsibility to maintain its membership roll according to its governing document.

The governing body of the Tribe will be either the current governing body when this law is passed, as described in the governing document, or any future governing body elected according to the election procedures in the governing document.

This law does not expand, reduce, or change any treaty rights or reserved rights that the Tribe may have.
